photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: beige-grey concrete or stone cladding at ground level with red-brown brick upper storeys
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Ground-floor storefronts heavily graffitied on windows and signage panels; upper brick facade appears intact from across-street view; round balconies in good condition
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor is all storefront glazing; upper floors have regular rectangular windows between round projecting balconies
photo_observations.door_details: Multiple commercial entries — Sahar International double-width entry, Yesrider entry with security gate, and likely a separate residential lobby
photo_observations.cornice_details: Flat parapet above top floor; horizontal string course/band divides commercial ground floor from upper residential/office levels
photo_observations.decorative_details: Round structural columns between storefronts; curved/round projecting balconies on upper floors give the building an Art Deco or early-modern character
photo_observations.storefront_description: Two distinct ground-floor commercial units: Sahar International (luggage, perfumes, Canada souvenirs with Canadian flag window posters) and Yesrider e-bike Parts & Service Center with security gate
photo_observations.signage_observed: SAHAR INTERNATIONAL (red/white with Canadian flags), YESRIDER Parts & Service Center, EMMO logo, BOOK AN APPOINTMENT, Canada Souvenirs, Luggage/Perfume/Mala/Maleta
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Modern commercial storefronts with contemporary signage; extensive graffiti on ground-floor glazing; security gate on Yesrider unit
photo_observations.notes: Night photos limit colour accuracy. Best views from IMG_20260316_003541905_HDR.jpg (across street) and IMG_20260316_003630342_HDR.jpg (Sahar storefront close-up).

facade_description: 438 Spadina Ave presents a street-facing facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a flat. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a beige-grey concrete or stone cladding at ground level with red-brown brick upper storeys palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 4 upper-window bays and 8 additional bays above, using mixed, and 3 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include string courses, cornice.
